programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan suspects a TV hostess of murdering a controversial radio presenter - but she seems to have the perfect alibi. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Morton Downey Jr and Vicki Lawrence

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The daughter of the attorney who defended Kimble at his trial tells him she has hired a private detective to help locate the missing one-armed killer

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An old flame of Trivette's appears asking for help, and Walker must come to the rescue

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A woman is killed shortly after participating in a charity event, and it emerges that she had tried to reveal the involvement of its hosts with terrorists. As the team delves deeper into her mysterious world, a plot is uncovered that could put the entire city in danger. Starring David Caruso

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. programme.desc: Stuart impresses his Sun Hill colleagues as he makes his debut on TV crime show Wanted, hoping to clear up a burglary case during which the victim, lawyer Ian Stroud, suffered a massive heart attack. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey kisses Sally - strictly in the course of his duties - as they trail bank robber Vince Murray to find out what he's up to back in England. Meanwhile, the old lag's son Darren proves to be a chip off the old block, staging a burglary with Ian Forrest's lad Kieran, but it's news to the youngsters that Ian was killed in the bank vault explosion while Vince survived the blast. Guest starring Nicky Henson and John Rogan

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Big surprises are in store for Peggy, whilst Nigel and Sanjay have a bet on the horses. Bianca's feeling rather low

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy is spreading gossip about Mark. Lorraine is back in the Square. Carol signs a petition against the Cobra Club, and April has an admirer

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Dave almost drowns during a beach landing exercise. Meanwhile, Donna's sister Kelly falls for one of the locals and Major Kieran Voce has divided loyalties over his love life

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe agrees to cook for a reluctant witness in a police safe house, and comes under threat from both a ruthless villain and an unscrupulous copper. Meanwhile, Henderson is alarmed by an unwelcome reminder of a past indiscretion. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan appears to have an ulterior motive for taking an interest in Tricky Woo's diet, and Mrs Pumphrey gets James to be the judge at the vegetable show. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko develops some outside interests - which only leads him into more trouble. Lionel loses his most treasured possession. Guest starring Pippa Haywood (Green Wing)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff of Gents' and Ladies' Ready-to-Wear volunteer to lead the fight against a takeover bid for Grace Brothers department store. Comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Trevor Bannister

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy takes Compo and Clegg on a nostalgic day out to the Keighley and Worth Valley Railway to ride on a steam train - but the trio soon interfere in matters that should not concern them. Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy hatches a plan to erect a flag in a prominent position - but first he needs to find one, and a pole to put it on. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: When her niece's son is charged with possession of an ecstasy tablet, suspicions remain that he has been framed, so Hetty goes undercover in his school as a cafeteria worker to clear his name. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam suspects a riot is in the offing when a football fan is killed. Knowing the problem will escalate as time goes by, the detective becomes determined to nip the violence in the bud, so he, Gene and Annie go undercover in a pub to catch the culprits. However, he realises the derby match that will be the focus of the conflict is the last one he went to with his dad when he was four years old

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Boyd receives a Nazi ceremonial dagger in an anonymous package in the post. The weapon prompts him to reopen the unsolved murder file of a girl whose body was found on the street in May 1945 - a case last examined by his former colleague Mel Silver shortly before her death. Feature-length crime drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey kisses Sally - strictly in the course of his duties - as they trail bank robber Vince Murray to find out what he's up to back in England. Meanwhile, the old lag's son Darren proves to be a chip off the old block, staging a burglary with Ian Forrest's lad Kieran, but it's news to the youngsters that Ian was killed in the bank vault explosion while Vince survived the blast. Guest starring Nicky Henson and John Rogan

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing 12th-century monk investigates a murder as the Abbey becomes involved in the war between King Stephen and the Empress Maud. The trail of clues leads him to a cache of hidden treasure and the fleeing daughter of the castle's owner, Godith. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: Billy and Heledd go head to head in a wheelchair rugby game. Plus, a chat with rugby coach Robin McBryde, Jonathan Davies reveals all about his team-mates, and Bala RFC are the squad

programme.title: Ffeil
programme.desc: Daily news and sport headlines aimed at a young audience

programme.title: Codi Pac
programme.desc: Geraint Hardy journeys around Wales, here talking to local people as he discovers what Llangollen has to offer in terms of places to stay, eat and see

programme.title: Be' Ti'n Gwylio?
programme.desc: Gwen and Ben, a mother and son from Ammanford, the Cambourne family from Nelson and a team of five Teulu'r Garth compete against each other from the comfort of their own homes for the chance to win a takeaway of their choice

programme.title: Newyddion S4C

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: Magazine programme, featuring stories from all over Wales

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Portreadau
programme.desc: Profile of late singer Aled Lloyd Davies, who enchanted audiences across Wales for many years with his singing voice. After retirement he kept busier than ever, playing his part in the musical world and especially in the world of cerdd dant. Adding to his interests in composing, gardening and writing, he was also a radio DJ

programme.title: Pobol y Cwm
programme.desc: As Gaynor endeavours to find out more about Izzy's monthly donation to Thailand, a hurt Izzy struggles to forgive her mother for suspecting her own daughter of fraud. An outraged Garry demands his credit card back, if Colin can remember where he kept it

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd

programme.title: Sioe Fach Fawr

programme.title: Caru Siopa
programme.desc: Lara Catrin is joined by best friends Dafydd and Gerwyn, who have to upcycle second-hand clothes and sell them on market stalls at the Maes in Caernarfon

programme.title: Fflam
programme.desc: Life begins to get better for Noni as her loving relationship with Deniz as they renovate their farmhouse and look forward to the future. Noni's best friend Malan and Deniz's sister Ekin are also full of hope as their plans to have a child together become possible, with Deniz agreeing to be a donor so he can be the father of their child. The future looks bright until Noni sees a ghost from her past - her husband who was killed in a terrible fire in Edinburgh, or at least man who looks very much like him. Drama, starring Gwyneth Keyworth, Richard Harrington and Memet Ali Alabora

programme.title: Noson Lawen
programme.desc: Nia Parry embarks on a romantic journey, in the company of Only Men Aloud, Elin Fflur, Al Lewis, Hywel Pitts, Wil Tân and his wife Ceri, Rhydian Jenkins, Meurig Thomas and the Brynhyfryd Trio

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of office space in Tring, Hertfordshire, a house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Lancashire and a semi-detached in Ambergate, Derby, after they are sold at auction

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Alex Mearns contemplates leaving life in suburbia behind and relocating to Cornwall, but his wife Robyn has reservations about leaving their families behind

programme.title: Critical Incident

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max attempt to find a masked assailant who stole the famous Icon of St Nicholas, but the escape of a person of interest results in a furious Ines removing the pair from the case, causing a rift between the two detectives. When the icon suddenly reappears, Miranda and Max must put their differences aside as they are drawn into a furious cat-and-mouse chase

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired couple find a characterful property in Norfolk, as well as learning about boat building in Wells-next-the-Sea and sailing a traditional yacht

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a clock made from the propeller of a First World War plane, a pair of stained glass windows, and an ancient shoe-stretching machine

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max reaches out to an unlikely source, causing him to break down, clearly unable to cope. Kathy feels lost with Ian still missing, so Bobby suggests she sees a doctor. A desperate Shirley and Mick learn that a woman matching Tina's description has been spotted and decide to take matters into their own hands

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha gets the shock of his life when a dangerous and vulnerable Jodie appears back on the scene. Kian's new boss Sahira breezes back into Holby, much to everyone's surprise. An outgoing new nurse has her first day working in a busy AAU, which proves to be a trial by fire

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from six of the fifth-round matches, including Everton v Tottenham Hotspur, Swansea City v Manchester City and Manchester United v West Ham United. Manchester City have once again avoided Premier League opposition in their quest to reach the quarter-finals, leaving much of the attention of the matches at Goodison Park and Old Trafford, which featured four sides in the top half of the top flight. Plus, highlights of Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Sheffield United v Bristol City and Burnley v Bournemouth

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel are still in with a chance of making the play-offs, but their last few matches are critical as they face off against some of the toughest teams in the league. Can the players, coaches Joey and Fabian rise to the challenge and prove they deserve to be in the top six? Patrik opens up about his issues with weight, and reveals that his parents have never come to see him play League of Legends.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore a large house in Dover in Kent, a house with issues in Camborne, Cornwall, and a two-bedroom flat in Woodford Green, Essex

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Ferris-Garbers yearn for the countryside on their doorstep and are hoping that a week in mum Caitlin's native Norfolk will convince their reluctant sons to leave London behind

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Newcastle and Humberside firefighters prepare for Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of year. In Birmingham a police officer is run over by the driver of a hijacked police car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams at the British Motor Museum in Warwick, and Natasha Raskin Sharp travels down the Stratford Canal with artist Dusty Miller

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Lily gets more than she bargained for, Aashiq is left with a bad taste in his mouth and Karen is in pain

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a buyer find a new countryside home in Cornwall, before she starts her new job in Truro, on a £260,000 budget. The presenter also investigates a campaign by surfers and divers to clean up the oceans

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: First-time farmers Paige and Kingsley are hoping that showing their sheep at the Isle of Wight show will bring them closer to their life dream of becoming full-time farmers

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Barnsley v Chelsea (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the concluding tie of the round, held at Oakwell. Championship outfit Barnsley have eliminated Norwich City and Tranmere Rovers so far, but now face a tough task against Thomas Tuchel's men, who have defeated Morecambe and Luton Town as they look to go one better than last season, when they were runners-up to Arsenal. Chelsea are the joint third most successful club in the history of this competition, having won it eight times. With analysis from Alan Shearer

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties in Worcestershire, South Croydon and Edgbaston, and follow investors hoping to make cash from their auction buys

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Michael Goodinson firmly believes his family's future lies in New Zealand, but wife Lisa sees things very differently. With major health concerns, time is not on Michael's side, so the clan embarks on a trial week sampling the life they could have 'Down Under'. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Ashford, Kent, a report of a burglary ends with a police officer fighting a man armed with a knife. Plus, other officers in the county get to grips with taser training

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles and experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique-hunting in Wrexham, north Wales, before heading to Stockport for the auction. Eric is also faced with a mystery where the answer might be under his nose

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit beaches and mountains in Thailand and discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade have helped create a rich and varied cuisine. They taste a traditional Thai breakfast and cook their own jungle curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to Dorset to help relocate a retired doctor and her husband who are selling up their Surrey family home. They have an £800,000 budget to spend on a spacious country property where they can enjoy their respective hobbies. Jonnie also heads out into the waters of West Bay to see how a conservation scheme is helping protect healthy levels of marine life and the livelihoods of local fishermen

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy takes action against Sharon, sending in the removal men and pleading with her to reveal what has happened to her son. Rainie confronts Max about his problems and tells him he is not fit to look after Abi. Mick goes to the police station to review his statement about Katy, while the truth comes out for Chelsea and Lucas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. Kush and Whitney have no plans for Valentine's Day, so they watch a movie together at home - but when there is a power cut, they find themselves getting much closer. Stuart convinces Keegan to buy Tiffany a bag - but Rainie points out it is not the expensive handbag his wife wanted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A stag do ends in tragedy when the host of a celebratory fishing trip is found harpooned on a beach the following morning. As the boat never moved, DI Neville Parker and his team struggle to determine how anyone in attendance could have been responsible. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, and comedians - and husband and wife - Marcus Brigstocke and Rachel Parris are appear. Plus, British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps a woman who wants to find a coastal retirement home in East Sussex, but her husband argues that she will regret moving away from her grandchildren

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion looks at the history of the East Grinstead Potter

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve presents highlights of his journeys around the world, and catches up with the people he met, including a Burmese undercover human rights campaigner, a homeless woman living in a Hollywood railway bridge, and a 10 year-old boy he met working in terrible conditions in a glass factory in Bangladesh

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright brings fitness to secondary school children, this time focusing on routines that get hearts pumping

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Your Questions Answered: Coronavirus
programme.desc: The Asian Network and BBC News Channel put viewers questions on coronavirus, vaccines and misinformation to England's Deputy Chief Medical Officer

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet and King Lear are dissected. Plus, chemistry and French

programme.title: Shakespeare's Mother: The Secret Life of a Tudor Woman
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Michael Wood charts the story of Mary Arden, the mother of William Shakespeare. Born during the reign of Henry VIII, she was the daughter of a Warwickshire farmer, and found herself part of the burgeoning middle class in Stratford-upon-Avon following her marriage to wool and hide dealer John Shakespeare. Together, they had eight children - three of whom died young - and her husband went on to become mayor before he was declared bankrupt, and they faced financial ruin, religious persecution and power politics - until they were rescued by their eldest son William

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: The team turns its attention to wool, which was the source of much of the country's wealth in Tudor times and had monasteries at the centre of its international trade. Peter Ginn builds a shearing bench, Tom Pinfold makes a salve out of broom, suet, brine and urine to seal any wounds that might occur while shearing, and Ruth Goodman uses sheep's milk to produce cheese to sell at market

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates royal food inspired by the days of India and Empire, as Paul Ainsworth cooks up quail and potato curry, said to be a favourite of Queen Victoria. Plus, historian Dr Annie Gray visits Osborne House on the Isle of Wight where Victoria, Empress of India, showcased her grand passion for the raj, and even hired her own Indian chefs

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers have to protect their pups from a deadly enemy, while Mozart and her sister Kinkajoo are forced to depend on one another for survival. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just as the annual lambing season gets into full swing, coronavirus grips the nation, and the farmers must work hard to keep their businesses afloat. In Aberdeenshire, new entrant farmers Isla and George lose their childcare, meaning Isla is left to look after the livestock and farm shop with three young children in tow. Elsewhere in the Lake District, Matt and Dani are faced with challenges when the usual team of students they rely on during their busiest time of year can no longer help with the calving

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. The first edition looks back over Trump's first year and a half in office, when he fell out spectacularly with some of America's oldest allies, and offers an inside look at crucial meetings and summits where the US President shocked his counterparts

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by star of The Mash Report Rachel Parris and actor, voiceover artist and stand-up Brennan Reece

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Investigators examine the home of a man covered in blood who hands himself into the police declaring he has murdered his wife, but who also claims to have no memory of the incident. Crime scene coordinator Jo Ward carries out analysis of the property in the hope of piecing together a sequence of events, but a crack in the man's mobile provides an insight into his motives

programme.title: Nadiya: Anxiety and Me
programme.desc: Since Nadiya Hussain won The Great British Bake Off in 2015, she's rarely been off TV screens. But behind the scenes Nadiya suffers from extreme anxiety and debilitating panic attacks, which she's had since childhood. For decades, she has kept her anxiety a secret, ashamed to speak out. Now, she sets out to find the cause of her anxiety, exploring the most effective, available treatments, while also having therapy herself, in the hope of managing her anxiety

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and the team present an edition focusing on wellbeing for both humans and animals, and how sometimes nature can have the answers. Megan McCubbin looks at the deep intelligence of pigeons, and why they should be admired, and there's a report from a woman who goes wild swimming with dolphins, something that has proved of enormous benefit to her mental health. Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative technology in Wales and Chris rounds up the latest footage from the cameras in the New Forest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Alex Mearns contemplates leaving life in suburbia behind and relocating to Cornwall, but his wife Robyn has reservations about leaving their families behind

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a recently retired Essex couple to look for a home in Staffordshire on their budget of £550,000, hoping to find a detached property with a view. Sonali also visits a celebrated Victorian pottery that is now a a hub of art, design and training

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to the last of the 16 dog groomers. First they tackle a quartet of cute Pomeranians, having to achieve a perfect pom-pom ball shape using scissors only. Then four party pooches hit the Dogwalk as a family of poodles and a Yorkshire terrier strut their stuff, having been given a makeover by the contestants. Who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor choose to go through to the quarter-finals?

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright brings fitness to secondary school kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions. Today he shows how to improve flexibility and balance

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Shakespeare's Macbeth, A Winter's Tale and Much Ado About Nothing. Plus, representation of data in maths

programme.title: How to Be Epic @ Everything
programme.desc: Experts demonstrate how to rap, act, sound like, dress like, and direct Shakespeare. With famous faces such as actress Maxine Peake, actor Ben Crystal, and Hip Hop Shakespeare Company's Lorianne Tika-Lemba

programme.title: Shakespeare Unlocked
programme.desc: Actors and directors from the Royal Shakespeare Company explore key scenes from the play, to unlock the meaning of language and development of character

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold prepare Tudor staples bread and ale, with Ruth trying to gather wild yeasts in the woods and Pete heading to a local windmill to help with the production of flour. They also join fellow parishioners in a traditional ceremony to pray for a good harvest and Peter and Tom assist farmer Neal Careswell as he weans the piglets

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: The teams exploit the landscape surrounding the farm as a way to generate more income. Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold mine lead in the Tudor fashion, while Ruth Goodman also uses the metal as she learns to make a stained-glass window. They also find out how aspirational tenant farmers would have decorated their farmhouses, with Ruth producing a wall hanging and Tom experimenting with a camera obscura to create a portrait of himself

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where items include an unusual French jardiniere and a silver tea service inscribed by football legend Stanley Matthews. Plus, a look at the work of photographer Patrick Lichfield

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In light of suggestions of 'truth warnings' for The Crown, and heated debate about colour-blind casting in Bridgerton, Mary talks to Line of Duty creator Jed Mercurio about how much fiction is needed to make fact into a hit TV drama. She also debates the perils of straddling the fact-fiction divide with writers James Graham and Simon Jenkins, and examines why this year's awards season features so many movies based on real-life events

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares another selection of her favourite dishes, beginning with a breakfast of fried bread and tomato hash inspired by her university days. A speedy meal comes in the form of a simple and tasty pasta dish with anchovies, tomatoes and mascarpone, while her friends are treated to a vegan feast with a butternut and sweet potato curry served with her coriander and jalapeno salsa, followed by a moreish lemon tendercake served with blueberry compote, which was prompted by a trip to Kansas City

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Neil and Clare have a garden with a stunning view of rural Cheshire, but they cannot decide how to turn the space into somewhere to benefit all of their large family. Two designers each come up with a new outdoor plan, pitching it to the couple through the use of virtual reality technology. But which one will they choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Jade Mountain, a hand-built hotel in St Lucia where each room has no outer wall and so is open to the ocean view - as well as having its own private infinity pool. Giles finds that room service here is tougher than it seems, and Monica dives for lionfish, which they both serve to guests visiting especially to have their wedding on the beach

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Performances by punk and new wave acts on the music show in 1977, including the Stranglers and the Jam, plus the continuing power of disco exemplified by Donna Summer's I Feel Love, the reggae of Bob Marley and pub rock by acts such as Eddie and the Hot Rods. Musicians including John Otway, JJ Burnel of the Stranglers, Paul Cook of the Sex Pistols, members of Darts and the Adverts reveal what it was like to appear on the programme in 1977

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. The first edition looks back over Trump's first year and a half in office, when he fell out spectacularly with some of America's oldest allies, and offers an inside look at crucial meetings and summits where the US President shocked his counterparts

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Donald Trump's last months as US president as he attempted to win, and subsequently overturn, the results of the 2020 election - culminating in scenes of violent 'insurrection' against the US Congress. Friends, advisers and close observers tell the story of Trump's last days, from packed rallies - held as coronavirus besieged America - to riots on Capitol Hill. The film provides a blow-by-blow account of the final weeks of his administration - and a gripping psychological study of the controversial leader's final days in power

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Investigation into the initial Covid-19 outbreak in Wuhan, examining the gulf between what the authorities knew and what was made available to the public. Over the course of 54 days, Chinese officials maintained that the outbreak was under control, with only a few cases linked to a live animal market. However, the infection soon proved to be far more widespread, necessitating stricter lockdown measures

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, getting a final look at the animals they've been witnessing for the last couple of weeks. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Two retired police officers with £600,000 explore the breathtaking landscapes of north Wales on a house-hunting expedition with presenter Jules Hudson. The couple want a traditional Welsh home with at least an acre of land. Jules also tests his nerve and takes the plunge when he experiences one of the region's many extreme watersports

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Guitar expert Julyan Wallis is presented with a cherished 1960s jazz guitar which has been left in a broken state for decades. Meanwhile, wood restoration expert Will Kirk has his work cut out when is presented with a handmade veneered card table. Later, the experts turn their attention to bookmakers bag and a Victorian clockwork pigeon

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright revisits his favourite exercises from the week

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: The playwright's use of relationships and historical figures in his works

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: This edition shows how the monasteries played a key role in providing welfare and charity for those in need. The team helps to restore a corrody room, which would have been granted to an elderly worker as a form of pension. Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold gather and roast limestone for the floor and Ruth Goodman harvests rushes from the river to make a mattress. They also help the abbot prepare for a big feast to entertain a patron, with Ruth tackling the laundry and Tom overseeing the production of a book to give as a gift

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold bring in the barley and celebrate with a harvest feast, while Ruth prepares for winter by salting meat to preserve it. As the team plans to put on a mystery play, Tom creates fireworks with an alchemist, while Ruth and Peter help with props and costumes. Preparing to leave the farm, they reflect on what they have learned and contemplate what the dissolution of the monasteries meant for the kingdom

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates food created for royal jubilees, with chef Anna Haugh using unusual 19th-century ingredients found in a dish created for Queen Victoria's golden one

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Hannibal's son Wilson leaves the Commandos to go in search of a female to start a family of his own, but his loyalty is later tested when the Whiskers attack his old clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Powderham Castle near Exeter, where Will Axon gets to grips with a mysterious wooden club and Claire Rawle is taken by a dove brooch. Presenter Paul Martin explores a lighthouse that moved from perilous rocks to Plymouth Hoe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in a bid to win a jackpot of £3,000

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a confit tomato and aubergine tarte tatin

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge research, a dedicated team of archaeologists has compiled the evidence to fill in a 400-year gap in man's knowledge of the bluestones of Stonehenge. By combining innovative 3D scanning techniques, traditional field archaeology and novel laboratory analysis, the team has discovered where the stones were quarried and where they first stood. Professor Alice Roberts shows how the team made its discoveries and joins Professor Mike Parker Pearson to piece together the final parts of the puzzle, solving one of the toughest challenges ever faced by archaeologists

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of roaming this week, with Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe and Benjamin Zephaniah and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark introduces analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Lady Macbeth (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman newly married to a rich industrialist in 19th-century England finds herself starved of companionship - so when her husband is called away to one of his collieries, she begins a passionate and fiery affair with a young groom from the estate. Drama, starring Florence Pugh, Cosmo Jarvis and Paul Hilton

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: D'Anna Biers is willing to take extreme measures to find out if Baltar is hiding information vital to the survival of the Cylons. Meanwhile, Admiral Adama and Roslin decide whether to put Apollo's plan into action and launch a biological weapon against their enemy. Sci-fi drama, starring James Callis and Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Three Cylon raiders are expected to mount an attack, but a desperate plea from one forces Starbuck to hold fire. Adama orders her to escort the pilot after learning his identity - Danny 'Bulldog' Novacek - a man taken prisoner three years ago while he and the Admiral were on a mission. Guilt-ridden Adama then struggles with the prospect of handing over the captive and aiding the conquest of the 12 colonies

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A look at the Bolton steeplejack's back garden, which housed one of the best examples of a steam-powered engineering workshop in the country, built using scrap from old mills and factories

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a purple night-sky scene featuring mountain overlooking a little falling river

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the east of Britain. Finds include new revelations from a 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village near Cambridgeshire, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri travel to Georgian mansion of Tatton Park in Cheshire, where Bendor has spotted a mysterious portrait of a 16th-century physician in its collection

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a purple night-sky scene featuring mountain overlooking a little falling river

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A look at the Bolton steeplejack's back garden, which housed one of the best examples of a steam-powered engineering workshop in the country, built using scrap from old mills and factories

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the east of Britain. Finds include new revelations from a 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village near Cambridgeshire, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver tries his hand at silent-movie directing as he films a scene from The Mayor of Casterbridge using an antique camera, to reveal how pioneers in Brighton taught the world about film-making long before Hollywood shot a frame

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack's interest in the machines of the Industrial Revolution, an enthusiasm that has helped to elevate the profile of this significant period in British history. Includes interviews with the Bolton man's family and friends

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape inspired by America's south west, featuring fresh skies above sun-dappled mountains and a silvery mere

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James discovers the downside of life as a vet when the post-mortem results for Lord Hulton's horse arrive. Siegfried's younger brother Tristan comes back from college and James is introduced to Mrs Pumphrey and her dog Tricki Woo

programme.title: The Lost City of Z (2016)
programme.desc: Drama telling the true story of British explorer Col Percy Fawcett, who in the 1920s told his fellows at the Royal Geographical Society in London he had found evidence of a secret city hidden away in the Amazon. But proving his theory meant returning yet again. Charlie Hunnam, Robert Pattinson and Sienna Miller star

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank embarks on a tour of more than 40 countries in search of the most beautiful man-made objects and structures the globe has to offer, including mysterious ruins, famous landmarks and the relics of civilisations long gone. The journey begins in South America, with visits to the fabled Inca city of Machu Picchu and the gigantic statue of Christ the Redeemer overlooking Rio de Janeiro

programme.title: Creating a Scene: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu presents a collection of dramatic short films from new British directors, with subjects ranging from speed dating and school friendships, to spotting UFOs

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape inspired by America's south west, featuring fresh skies above sun-dappled mountains and a silvery mere

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri travel to Georgian mansion of Tatton Park in Cheshire, where Bendor has spotted a mysterious portrait of a 16th-century physician in its collection

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on August 16, with performances by Go West, Sting, the KLF, New Kids on the Block, Together, Betty Boo, Jon Bon Jovi, Roxette, Partners in Kryme and Primal Scream

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Celebrating some of the decade's most recognisable classics, including performances by the Carpenters, Bread, Charles Aznavour, 10cc, John Denver, the Bellamy Brothers, Exile, Captain & Tennille and Dr Hook

programme.title: Love Songs at the BBC: A Valentine's Day Special
programme.desc: A selection of romantic songs for guilty pleasures, for courtship, for declarations of love, for looking someone in the eye and whispering sweet nothings, accompanied by a compilation of some of the greatest love songs from the likes of Celine Dion, Joe Cocker and Jennifer Warnes, Jason and Kylie, 10cc and Lionel Richie, all from the Top of the Pops era

programme.title: Roy Orbison: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Documentary revealing Orbison's legacy as a beloved rock legend and a devoted father through intimate interviews with his three sons. Alex, Roy Jr and Wesley Orbison discuss the immense talent and fierce determination that provided the driving force behind their father's success and how his family life offered a strong spiritual base to escape the pressures of the rock 'n' roll lifestyle

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at The Bridge, by Marley Marl and MC Shan. The song, designed to foster community pride, ignited hip-hop's most epic rap battle as contributors including Roxanne Shante, DJ Red Alert, Funkmaster Flex and the artists themselves recall

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at Queen Latifah's anthem of women empowerment, 1989's Ladies First. Sexism was endemic in the hip-hop sector of the music industry during the 1980s, and Latifah recalls the lack of money put towards female rappers for marketing and music videos compared to their male counterparts

programme.title: More Dangerous Songs: And the Banned Played On
programme.desc: A compilation of songs previously banned from broadcast on the BBC, including the Kinks' Lola, Jackie by Scott Walker, and (We Don't Need This) Fascist Groove Thing by Heaven 17

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music public relations expert Alan Edwards tells the story of why so many bands are getting back together, what happens when they do - and how such reunions have changed the music business. He starts by recalling the UK's first revival concert, which took place over 40 years ago at Wembley Stadium. Featuring Bill Hailey and the Comets, Little Richard and Jerry Lee Lewis - the event opened the eyes of promoters to the power of yesterday's hit-makers to reach an audience and make money. From there, Alan takes on a musical journey through some of the biggest reunions of the last 30 years. Contributors include Glen Matlock, Debbie Harry, Stewart Copeland and Melanie C

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Singer, DJ and broadcaster Cerys Matthews joins acclaimed blues photographer Val Wilmer to select their favourite blues musicians - several of whom Val has met and photographed. From Muddy Waters and Howlin' Wolf to Jimi Hendrix and John Lee Hooker, Sister Rosetta Tharpe to Peggy Lee and many more, their playlist is packed with classic blues and punctuated with personal stories

programme.title: Roy Orbison: Love Hurts

Alex, Roy Jr and Wesley Orbison discuss the immense talent and fierce determination that provided the driving force behind their father's success and how his family life offered a strong spiritual base to escape the pressures of the rock 'n' roll lifestyle

programme.desc: Mimi is on a mission to debunk stereotypes by showing that you don’t have to be just one thing in life. We also meet Nora, who has albinism and is living her dream as a fashion model. And Mimi hosts her Q&A with SBTV creator Jamal Edwards MBE, who shares his own journey to success, and how he has helped the likes of Ed Sheeran. Mimi’s family and friends make a surprise appearance for the end of the series and offer words of wisdom for anyone wanting to chase their own dreams.

But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit beaches and mountains in Thailand and discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade have helped create a rich and varied cuisine. They taste a traditional Thai breakfast and cook their own jungle curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to Dorset to help relocate a retired doctor and her husband who are selling up their Surrey family home. They have an £800,000 budget to spend on a spacious country property where they can enjoy their respective hobbies. Jonnie also heads out into the waters of West Bay to see how a conservation scheme is helping protect healthy levels of marine life and the livelihoods of local fishermen

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy takes action against Sharon, sending in the removal men and pleading with her to reveal what has happened to her son. Rainie confronts Max about his problems and tells him he is not fit to look after Abi. Mick goes to the police station to review his statement about Katy, while the truth comes out for Chelsea and Lucas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. Kush and Whitney have no plans for Valentine's Day, so they watch a movie together at home - but when there is a power cut, they find themselves getting much closer. Stuart convinces Keegan to buy Tiffany a bag - but Rainie points out it is not the expensive handbag his wife wanted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A stag do ends in tragedy when the host of a celebratory fishing trip is found harpooned on a beach the following morning. As the boat never moved, DI Neville Parker and his team struggle to determine how anyone in attendance could have been responsible. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: The Paddy Raff Show
programme.desc: More sketches featuring the Belfast stand-up comedian, showcasing his unique and quirky take on modern life. In this episode, Granny Raff gets a surprise Valentine's Day visitor, risking the attention of her nosey neighbours. Father Pat's interdenominational all-request mass puts one foot in the rave with an uplifting service of remembrance. DJ Morgan K still has no time for listener requests on Belfast's most talked about radio station, Bel-FM, while newsreader Henry Campbell offers a retrospective view of the latest Covid stories - 1990s style

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, and comedians - and husband and wife - Marcus Brigstocke and Rachel Parris are appear. Plus, British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: The Exiles: Hong Kong at a Crossroads
programme.desc: In June 2020, after months of increasingly violent protests in Hong Kong, Beijing imposed a sweeping national security law. Critics say it effectively curtails protest and freedom of speech - China has said it will return stability. In response, the UK has introduced a new visa that will give 70% of its former colony's population - 5.4m people - the right to live in the UK, and eventually become citizens. Many activists now face an agonising choice - to stay or to go.

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Lucy Hedges looks at Dubai's efforts to reschedule its mammoth 2020 World Expo, which was postponed during the pandemic. The team also catches up with Cuba's enigmatic shoeshine poet, checks out the typewriter tycoon whose fortunes changed one part of northern Italy and meets the South Korean robot that can peer into the future.

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod is in Geirinis sorting out a problem with rising damp. Florence is considering demolishing her garden shed as all the material she stores is soaking wet because of moisture coming through the walls. After clearing the space, Donnie can get cracking on his planned solution of a raised floor. The presenter also visits Cothrom ReStore in Ormacleit and tries his hand at upcycling furniture and the work continues on the Caladh Trust kitchen renovation in Benbecula

programme.title: Eòrpa (European Current Affairs)
programme.desc: Annabel Maclennan presents a selection of Eòrpa reports from previous years connected to Europe's coasts and seas. programme.desc: A chance to meet Coinneach Clogaid and Olaidh Orains, and we come upon an exclamation mark in a strange place

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: When Rudeigin and Rob appear at the Mùman house, Mùmantrobha is not at all happy with them as he feels jealousy towards them

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Esther from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies, which include playing the piano and archery

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: The town council investigates when a visitor, whose features and behaviour strike some residents as familiar, makes his presence known in the community

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison recalls the Van Leer Project in Uist, and the unusual way news of plans to build a rocket range on South Uist was discovered

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: It's nearing the end of the season, but there's still lots going on in the urban garden. Joy Dunlop comes to visit, and samples some homegrown treats

programme.title: Beloved Wilderness
programme.desc: Documentary about the work of the Highlands and Islands Development Board in the 1960s, as Robert Grieve's five-year tenure as chairman came to an end. Plus, a look at the schemes passed by the Board throughout that time

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Kris Drever's solo performance, including ‘Freedom Come A' Ye', from Na Trads in 2019

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Ingrid plans a party for Fredrik's 50th birthday and breaks social rules to support a friend in trouble. Christian and the other divers at the Ocean Viking are taking risks every day at work, where things are getting increasingly dangerous. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, heading to the coastal region of Sanna, at the far western tip of the peninsula of Ardnamurchan, in Lochaber

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic
programme.desc: A compilation programme from Celtic Connections 2021

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Cumbrian couple Kevin and Michelle, flatmates Shantelle and Vanessa, and sporty partners Gemma and James use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Kieran, Jo, Kimberley and Gary pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will has a choice to make, Noah goads Charity, and Rhona and Marlon enjoy their secret

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is concerned about Johnny's mental health when he claims he can see Aidan. Abi tells Kevin how Debbie saved her life, but ended up killing Ray. Fiz admonishes Tyrone for throwing Alina out at such short notice

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter hikes seven and a half miles of the wild Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall's most southerly point, starting at the much-photographed Kynance Cove and finishing at the idyllic fishing village of Cadgwith. Along the way she learns about Serpentine Rock, visits a hut that changed the course of modern technology, and gets a pasty making master class

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin confides in Abi that he suspects Debbie is lying, and guesses Ray is still alive. Johnny promises Carla he will talk to the prison doctor about his hallucinations, as long as she doesn't tell Jenny

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Following an unexpected turn in the case, Lisa and the team struggle to keep their heads in the game as they are forced to accept help from a new unit. As one suspect throws new light on the investigation, Lisa doubts whether she has been given the full truth on events from the past. With the investigative pressure building, Manning attempts to fight for his marriage, and Lisa finds solace in a place she least expected

programme.desc: Piers welcomes Gemma Collins for an emotional, surprising and hilarious interview. She also talks about living with fellow The Only Way is Essex star Arg and how she spends the money she has made

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 63 Up
programme.desc: Director Michael Apted catches up with more of the people he first met as seven-year-old children back in 1964. Paul and Symon - who back then were filmed growing up in the same children's home - have an emotional reunion in Australia, where Paul emigrated as a teenager. The film also catches up with Neil, whose rollercoaster life has taken him from homelessness to politics

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: The widow is struggling to sleep, growing short of money, and Gerry has just accepted an offer on the house behind her back. In order to stop her home from being sold from under her, Alice must find a way to disrupt the sale and make big money fast. While Charlotte gets to know George, Alice uncovers a deed for land in Harry's name and wonders if this could be the lifeline she's been hoping for. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Donnie, Kate, Jack and Nicole work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas has a proposition for Marlon, Paul is on edge, and Mandy is troubled by the feeling that something is not right

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Debbie get trapped in the Bistro fridge, and Johnny tells Carla that the doctor is referring him to a psychiatrist. Tyrone realises he has been duped while looking after the furniture shop for Fiz, and Leanne books a consultation with a TV psychic

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison
